info.name: Lead Generation Platform — Phase 2 & 3
info.description:
Complete API collection for **Phase 2 — Leads CRM Engine** and **Phase 3 — Capture Engine**.

---

## Authentication
All endpoints require a session cookie (`Cookie: __session=<value>`) except:
- `POST /capture/forms/:formId/submit` — fully public

Run **🔐 Setup → 1. Register** then **🔐 Setup → 2. Login** to get a session. The Login request automatically saves the cookie to the `sessionCookie` variable.

---

## Collection Variables

| Variable | Description | Auto-set by |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| `baseUrl` | API base URL | _(set manually)_ |
| `sessionCookie` | Full `__session=xxx` cookie string | Login |
| `leadId` | Last created lead UUID | Capture Lead |
| `formId` | Last created form UUID | Create Form |
| `listId` | Last created list UUID | Create List |
| `eventId` | Last created badge event UUID | Create Badge Event |
| `attendeeId` | First attendee UUID from last upload | Upload Attendees |

---

## Example Flows
See the **⚡ Flows** folder for guided end-to-end sequences. Run each folder's requests top-to-bottom.

---

## Error Codes Reference

| Code | HTTP | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| `LEAD_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Lead ID doesn't exist or belongs to another user |
| `DUPLICATE_LEAD` | 409 | Email already captured by this user |
| `BLACKLISTED_LEAD` | 422 | Email/domain is on the user's blacklist |
| `FORM_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Form ID not found or wrong owner |
| `ALREADY_SCANNED` | 409 | Badge attendee already scanned |
| `INVALID_FORM_OPERATION` | 409 | Lifecycle transition not allowed (e.g. delete active form) |
| `VALIDATION_ERROR` | 400 | Request body failed schema validation |

variable.description: Auto-set by Upload Attendees (first in array)

item.name: 🔐 Setup — Authenticate
item.description:
⚠️ **Email verification is enforced.** Login returns `401 UNVERIFIED_EMAIL` if the account has not been verified.

Run requests **1 → 2 → 3** in order:
1. Register — creates the account and triggers a verification email
2. Verify Email — submit the 6-digit code from the email → **this sets the session cookie** (you can skip step 3 after a fresh verify)
3. Login — use for subsequent sessions after the cookie expires

For local dev, watch the server stdout for a line like:
```
[email-verification] code=847291 → demo@example.com
```
or point `SMTP_HOST` at Mailpit/MailHog to capture outbound mail.

request.description:
Creates a new user account. Returns `201`:
```json
{
  "userId": "uuid",
  "username": "demouser",
  "message": "Verification code sent to your email"
}
```

A 6-digit verification code is immediately sent to the registered email. You **must** submit that code via **POST /auth/verify-email** before login is possible — attempting to log in before verification returns `401 UNVERIFIED_EMAIL`.

**Rate limit:** 10 requests / minute per IP.

exec: // verify-email creates a session immediately — capture the cookie here
console.log('✅ sessionCookie saved from verify-email:', match[1].substring(0, 30) + '...');
console.warn('⚠️  No Set-Cookie — wrong code or already verified');

request.description:
Submits the 6-digit code from the verification email. Replace `847291` with the actual code.

**On success (200):** the server immediately creates a session and sets the `__session` cookie — you are logged in and can skip step 3.

**The Test script** auto-saves the cookie to `sessionCookie`.

**Errors:**
- `400 INVALID_CODE` — wrong or expired code
- `404` — email not found

Codes are single-use and expire after 15 minutes. Use **POST /auth/resend-verification** if the code expires.

Sends a fresh 6-digit code if the original expired or was not received. Only needed if step 2 fails with an expired code error.

Returns `200 { message: 'Verification code resent' }`.

exec: // Extract the session cookie from the Set-Cookie response header
console.warn('⚠️  No Set-Cookie — check credentials, or account may not be verified yet (run step 2 first)');

request.description:
Authenticates a **verified** account. On success the server sets an `HttpOnly` session cookie.

⚠️ Returns `401 UNVERIFIED_EMAIL` if step 2 has not been completed.

The **Test** script auto-saves the cookie to `sessionCookie`. All subsequent authenticated requests send `Cookie: {{sessionCookie}}`.

**Rate limit:** 5 requests / minute per IP.

item.name: ⚡ Flows
item.description: Guided end-to-end sequences. Run each sub-folder top-to-bottom for a working demo. All flows assume you have already run 🔐 Setup.

item.name: Flow A — Manual Lead Journey
item.description:
Captures a lead manually, enriches it with a tag and a note, advances the status through the pipeline, then cleans up.

1. Capture Lead → saves `leadId`
2. Add Tag
3. Move to _contacted_
4. Add a note
5. Inspect the full lead record
6. Delete lead

body.raw: { "body": "Spoke with Sarah on 2026-04-12 — strong interest in the enterprise plan. Follow up after Q2 budget review." }

item.name: Flow B — Form → Public Submit → Export
item.description:
Creates a lead capture form, publishes it, submits it as a public (anonymous) visitor, then exports all leads as CSV.

1. Create Form → saves `formId`
2. Publish Form (makes it live)
3. Submit Form (public — no Cookie header)
4. List Leads (confirm submission created a lead)
5. Export CSV

request.description:
Creates a new form in `draft` status. The `fields` array supports: `text`, `email`, `phone`, `company`, `dropdown`, `checkbox`, `file`.
`qualificationConfig` is an optional map — each entry adds to the lead's `qualificationScore` when the form is submitted.
Transitions the form from `draft` → `active`. After this the public submit endpoint is live. Returns the updated form object.

exec: if (r.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('leadId', r.id); console.log('leadId from form submit =', r.id); }

request.description:
⚠️ **This is the only public endpoint in the system — no Cookie header required.**

The server looks up the form by ID, validates it is `active`, creates a lead under the form owner's account, and atomically increments `submission_count` on the form.

Sending to a `draft` or `archived` form returns `404 FORM_NOT_FOUND`.

Returns `text/csv` with `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="leads.csv"`.

Columns: `email, firstName, lastName, phone, company, jobTitle, status, captureMethod, capturedAt`

item.name: Flow C — Badge Event → Scan
item.description:
Creates a badge event (e.g. a conference), bulk-uploads the attendee list, then simulates scanning a badge to create a lead.

1. Create Badge Event → saves `eventId`
2. Upload Attendees → saves `attendeeId` (first attendee)
3. Scan Badge → creates a lead, stamps `scanned_at` on the attendee row
4. Get Event (verify scan)

exec: // Response is { inserted: N } — run C2b next to fetch attendee IDs

request.description: Bulk-uploads the conference attendee list. Duplicate emails within the same event are silently skipped (`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`). Returns `{ inserted: N }` — run C2b next to fetch attendee IDs.
Fetches all attendees for the event. The Test script saves `r[0].id` as `attendeeId` — the first attendee (Priya Sharma) will be scanned in C3.

exec: console.log('Lead created from scan — leadId =', r.lead.id);

request.description:
Scans a badge. The server:
1. Verifies you own the event
2. Checks the attendee belongs to this event
3. Guards against double-scan → `409 ALREADY_SCANNED`
4. Creates a lead under your account with `captureMethod = 'badge_scan'`
5. Stamps `scanned_at` + `lead_id` on the attendee row

Returns `{ lead, attendee }`.

item.name: C4 · Get Badge Event (verify attendee stamped)
Flow D — CSV Import
item.description:
Demonstrates all three CSV deduplication modes.

1. Import with `skip` — first-time bulk import
2. Import same file with `merge` — only non-empty new fields overwrite existing data
3. Export all leads to CSV

**CSV format for the file upload** (save as `leads.csv`):
```
email,first_name,last_name,company,job_title
alice@demo.com,Alice,Walker,DemoCo,Founder
bob@demo.com,Bob,Martinez,BetaLabs,CTO
carol@demo.com,Carol,Kim,Gamma Inc,Head of Sales
```

The `columnMap` query param tells the importer how your CSV headers map to internal field names.

formdata.description: Select your CSV file. Expected headers: email, first_name, last_name, company, job_title

query.description: JSON map of internal field names to CSV column headers

request.description:
Multipart file upload. Select a CSV file in Postman's `Body → form-data`.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "imported": 3,
  "skipped": 0,
  "failed": 0,
  "errors": []
}
```

**dedupMode=skip** — if a row's email is already captured, that row is counted as `skipped` and the existing lead is left unchanged.

item.name: D2 · Import CSV (merge mode — re-import same file)

query.description: Only non-empty CSV fields overwrite existing lead data

request.description:
**dedupMode=merge** — for every duplicate email, only _non-empty_ fields from the CSV row overwrite the existing lead. Empty CSV cells leave the existing value untouched.

Useful for enrichment imports: add phone/company without wiping existing data.

Returns the full lead database as a CSV download (up to 10,000 rows).

**Headers:** `email, firstName, lastName, phone, company, jobTitle, status, captureMethod, capturedAt`

item.name: Phase 2 — Leads CRM
item.description: All endpoints are under the `/leads` prefix and require an authenticated session cookie.

item.name: Blacklist
item.description: Manage email and domain blocklist. Any capture attempt (form submit, badge scan, CSV import, manual capture) against a blacklisted email or any email on a blacklisted domain returns `422 BLACKLISTED_LEAD`.

request.description:
Blacklists a specific email address. Returns `201` with the created entry.

`type` must be `"email"` or `"domain"`.
Blacklists an entire domain. Any email `@spamcorp.io` will be rejected at capture time.

URL-encode the value if it contains special characters (e.g. `@`).
Returns `204 No Content` on success.

item.name: Lists (Segments)
item.description: Static lead lists — named segments you can bulk-populate and use for targeted outreach. Lists are independent of lead status/tags.

request.description:
Bulk-adds leads to a list. All `leadIds` must belong to your account. Returns `{ ok: true }` on success.

You can pass multiple UUIDs: `"leadIds": ["uuid1", "uuid2", "uuid3"]`
Deletes the list and cascade-removes all list memberships. The leads themselves are NOT deleted. Returns `204 No Content`.

item.name: Leads
item.description: Core lead CRUD. Every operation is scoped to the authenticated user — you cannot read or modify another user's leads.

body.raw:
{
  "email": "alexis.dupont@enterprise.fr",
  "firstName": "Alexis",
  "lastName": "Dupont",
  "phone": "+33 1 42 00 00 00",
  "company": "Enterprise FR",
  "jobTitle": "Chief Digital Officer",
  "captureMethod": "manual",
  "captureSourceId": "trade-show-berlin-2026",
  "utmSource": "tradeshow",
  "utmMedium": "offline",
  "utmCampaign": "emea-q2-2026",
  "utmContent": "booth-scan",
  "qualificationScore": 90,
  "qualificationAnswers": {
    "budget": "€100k+",
    "timeline": "Q4 2026",
    "painPoint": "Manual lead management",
    "decisionMaker": true
  }
}

request.description:
Creates a lead with all optional fields populated. Returns `201` with the full lead object.

**Status pipeline:** `new → contacted → qualified → meeting_booked → won → lost` (default is `new`)

**Returns `409 DUPLICATE_LEAD`** if this email is already captured by your account.
Only `email` is required. All other fields are optional and default to `null`.

query.description: Filter: new | contacted | qualified | meeting_booked | won | lost

request.description: Returns `{ leads: Lead[], total: number }`. Use `status` and `captureMethod` query params to filter. Disabled params are shown as examples — enable them to filter.
Partial update — only send fields you want to change. The `email` field is **immutable** (not accepted here). `status` must be one of: `new | contacted | qualified | meeting_booked | won | lost`.
Hard delete. Cascades to tags, notes, and list memberships. Returns `204 No Content`.

item.name: Tags
item.description: Per-lead tags. Operations are idempotent — adding an existing tag is a no-op.

request.description: Adds a tag to the lead. Returns `{ ok: true }`. Adding a tag that already exists is silently idempotent.
Removes the tag from the lead. Returns `204 No Content`. Replace `enterprise` in the URL with the actual tag value.

item.name: Notes
item.description: Free-form text notes attached to a lead. Notes are append-only and ordered by creation time.

body.raw: { "body": "Discovery call completed 2026-04-12. Strong fit for the Enterprise plan — follows up in 2 weeks after internal review with CFO." }

request.description: Appends a note to the lead. Returns `201` with the created note object including `id`, `body`, `createdAt`, `createdBy`.

item.name: Phase 3 — Capture Engine
item.description: All endpoints are under the `/capture` prefix. The only unauthenticated endpoint is `POST /capture/forms/:formId/submit`.

item.name: Forms
item.description: Form lifecycle: `draft → active → archived`. You can only delete a `draft` form. Publishing makes the public submit endpoint live.

request.description:
Creates a form in `draft` status. The `name` field is trimmed and required. `fields` is a free-form JSONB array — the backend stores whatever field definitions you provide.

Returns `201` with the full form object including `id`, `status: 'draft'`, `submissionCount: 0`.
Returns all forms for the authenticated user. Optionally filter by `?status=draft|active|archived`.
Partial update. Send only fields to change. All fields are optional.

Transitions `draft → active`. After this, `POST /capture/forms/:formId/submit` is live for anonymous visitors.

Returns the updated form object with `status: 'active'`.
Archives a form (any status → `archived`). The public submit endpoint will immediately return `404` after archiving. Data is preserved.

Deletes the form. Only allowed on `draft` forms — attempting to delete an `active` form returns `409 INVALID_FORM_OPERATION`.

Returns `204 No Content`.

header.description: The public-facing base URL. The QR encodes: {baseUrl}/f/{formId}

request.description: Returns a PNG QR code as a Base64 data URL. **Response:** ```json { "qrCode": "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo..." } ``` The QR encodes `{x-base-url}/f/{formId}`. Set the `x-base-url` header to your public domain. Defaults to `https://app.example.com` if omitted. Paste the `qrCode` value into an `` tag or use it directly in a PDF/print template.

⚠️ **No session cookie required.**

This endpoint is intended for anonymous website visitors submitting an embedded form. The server resolves the form owner from the form record — the submitter never provides a `userId`.

**Errors:**
- `404 FORM_NOT_FOUND` — form is `draft` or `archived`
- `422 BLACKLISTED_LEAD` — the email is on the form owner's blacklist
- `409 DUPLICATE_LEAD` — email already captured by the form owner

item.name: Badge Events
item.description: Model conference / trade show badge scanning. Create an event, bulk-upload the attendee list, then scan badges on the show floor to instantly create qualified leads.

request.description:
Bulk-creates attendee records for the event. Duplicate emails within the same event are silently skipped.

Returns `201` with the array of created attendees. The test script saves `r[0].id` to `attendeeId` for use in the Scan Badge request.
Returns all attendees for the event, ordered by upload order (ASC). Scanned attendees will have `scannedAt` and `leadId` populated.

exec: console.log('Lead created from badge scan — leadId =', r.lead.id);

request.description:
Scans a badge. The server executes atomically:
1. Verify you own this event
2. Verify the attendee belongs to this event
3. Guard against double-scan → `409 ALREADY_SCANNED`
4. Create a lead: `captureMethod = 'badge_scan'`, `captureSourceId = eventId`
5. Stamp `scanned_at + lead_id` on the attendee row

**Response:**
```json
{
  "lead": { "id": "uuid", "email": "...", "captureMethod": "badge_scan", ... },
  "attendee": { "id": "uuid", "scannedAt": "2026-04-12T...", "leadId": "uuid", ... }
}
```
Deletes the event and cascade-removes all attendee records. The leads created from badge scans are **not** deleted. Returns `204 No Content`.

item.name: CSV Import / Export
item.description: Bulk import leads from a CSV file with configurable column mapping and deduplication. Export all leads as a downloadable CSV.

formdata.description:
CSV file. Prepare a file with headers matching your columnMap below.

Example content:
email,first_name,last_name,company,job_title
jane@example.com,Jane,Doe,Acme Corp,VP Sales
john@example.com,John,Smith,BetaTech,CTO

query.description:
JSON object. Keys = internal field names, values = CSV header names.
Available internal fields: email, firstName, lastName, phone, company, jobTitle

request.description:
**Multipart file upload.** In Postman: Body tab → form-data → add key `file` (type = File) → select your CSV.

**dedupMode options:**
- `skip` — existing emails are counted as `skipped`, no changes made
- `overwrite` — all fields from CSV row replace the existing lead
- `merge` — only non-empty CSV fields overwrite; blank cells leave existing data

**Response:**
```json
{
  "imported": 45,
  "skipped":  3,
  "failed":   1,
  "errors": [
    { "row": 12, "email": "bad@@email", "error": "Invalid email" }
  ]
}
```

Blacklisted emails are counted as **skipped** (not failed).
**dedupMode=overwrite** — for duplicate emails, ALL fields from the CSV row replace the existing lead record. Use when you have a complete, authoritative updated export from another system.

**dedupMode=merge** — for duplicate emails, only non-empty CSV fields are written. This is ideal for enrichment imports: add phone numbers to existing leads without wiping their names, status, or tags.

The `columnMap` here only maps `phone` and `company` — only those fields will be updated on matches.

Downloads all leads as a CSV file. Postman will show the raw CSV body — click **Save Response** to download the file.

**Response headers:**
```
Content-Type: text/csv; charset=utf-8
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="leads.csv"
```

**CSV columns (always in this order):**
```
email, firstName, lastName, phone, company, jobTitle, status, captureMethod, capturedAt
```

Up to 10,000 rows per export.
